The Senecio genus is a group of flowering plants in the daisy family (Asteraceae), which includes various species with medicinal properties. Senecio species, such as Senecio ilkasiensis, have been used in traditional medicine to treat various ailments, including fever, rheumatism, and respiratory issues. The bioactive constituents of these plants typically include sesquiterpene lactones, flavonoids, and alkaloids, which may contribute to their medicinal properties. Senecio species are often used to make teas, infusions, and tinctures, which are among the most common herbal preparations made with these plants.
